channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Syrian leader signs constitution that puts the country under an Islamist group's rule for 5 years
item.description: Syria’s interim president has signed a temporary constitution that leaves the country under Islamist rule for five years during a transitional phase.
item.dc:creator: Ghaith Alsayed And Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UN report accuses Israel of sexual violence against Palestinians. Netanyahu claims anti-Israel bias
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — United Nations-backed human rights experts on Thursday accused Israel of “the systematic use of sexual, reproductive and other gender-based violence” in its war with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Study says endangered Asian elephant population in Cambodia is more robust than previously thought
item.description: A genetic study of Asian elephants in northern Cambodia reveals a larger and more robust population than previously thought, raising hopes the endangered species could slowly recover.
item.dc:creator: Sopheng Cheang And David Rising, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ethiopia's Tigray regional government appeals for help after factional fighters seize key towns
item.description: ADDIS ABABA, Ethiopia (AP) — The interim government of Ethiopia’s Tigray region appealed for the Ethiopian federal government to intervene after a faction of the Tigray People's Liberation Front seized control of two major towns, leaving several people wounded and raising fears of a return to civil
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL GETACHEW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Naples struck by 4.4-magnitude quake causing minor damage and light injuries
item.description: ROME (AP) — The southern Italian city of Naples was struck by a 4.4-magnitude quake early Thursday that caused only minor damage and sent 11 people to the hospital, the most serious suffering contusions after part of a ceiling collapsed, officials said.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia presents US with demands that need to be met before ending Ukraine war: report
item.description: Russia has reportedly laid out its demands for ending the war in Ukraine and they mirror previous requests made to Kyiv, the U.S. and NATO.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US envoy visits Moscow for ceasefire talks that a Russian official says would help Ukraine
item.description: Russia claimed Thursday that its troops have driven the Ukrainian army out of a key town in Russia’s Kursk border region, as a senior Kremlin official said that a U.S.-proposed 30-day ceasefire in the three-year war on Ukraine would help Kyiv by giving its military a break.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pakistan says insurgents who attacked a passenger train killed 21 hostages while all others freed
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Insurgents who attacked a passenger train carrying 440 passengers in restive southwestern Pakistan killed 21 hostages before security forces killed all 33 of the assailants, and all other passengers were rescued, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump envoys begin Russia outreach after Ukraine breakthrough
item.description: Trump administration officials are reaching out to their Russian counterparts as the White House says the ball is in Moscow’s court to agree to a ceasefire in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: US puts Putin in hot seat with Ukraine ceasefire proposal
item.description: The United States has for the first time shifted the pressure to Russia in President Trump’s push for a ceasefire in Ukraine, after securing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s support for a 30-day truce in the war.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: NYC mayoral candidate confronts Trump border czar over ICE arrest of Columbia graduate
item.description: New York State Assembly member and mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani confronted President Trump's border czar, Tom Homan, in Albany on Wednesday over the recent arrest of Columbia University graduate and green card holder Mahmoud Khalil by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Pressed to accept a ceasefire in Ukraine, Putin seems likely to seek his own conditions
item.description: By signaling its openness to a ceasefire, Ukraine has handed the Kremlin a difficult challenge at a time when the Russian military has the upper hand in the war: Should Moscow accept a truce and abandon hopes of making new gains, or should it reject the offer and risk derailing a cautious rapprochem
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills 8 Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas says ceasefire talks resume
item.description: Israeli fire has killed eight Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and three more in the occupied West Bank over the past 24 hours, Palestinian officials said Tuesday, even as a fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza has largely held since late January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Canada suspends 25% electricity tariffs on US
item.description: This comes after what Ford said was a "productive" conversation with U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Evangelical leader says US must protect Syrian Christians from attacks by jihadi terrorists
item.description: A senior evangelical leader has called on the U.S. to not recognize new Syrian regime following bloodshed of religious minorities, including Christians and Alawites, carried out by radical Islamists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Search for Sudiksha Konanki, missing Pitt student, continues in Dominican Republic
item.description: Sudiksha Konanki was last seen early Thursday morning on a beach near Punta Cana. Police have spoken with a man allegedly with her at the time.
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith, NewsNation

item.title: British tourist detained in ICE center after visa suspension: Family
item.description: In a statement to NewsNation, an ICE spokesperson said Burke was detained by the agency "for violation of the terms and conditions of her admission.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: US, Ukraine talk peace as Kyiv, Moscow trade drone attacks
item.description: Officials from Ukraine and the United States have gathered in Saudi Arabia to discuss a potential limited ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Greenland’s opposition party pushes closer US ties, independence from Denmark as Trump plays big in election
item.description: Greenland holds a parliamentary vote in an election that will likely reveal how the nation will seek independence from Denmark as well its response to President Trump's ambitions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US-Canada products are 99% tariff-free: Canadian ambassador to US
item.description: Canada's ambassador to the United States said 99% of products traded between the two nations are tariff-free.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctors declare Pope Francis no longer in imminent danger due to pneumonia, but remains hospitalized
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors said Monday Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a month, but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Korea fires missiles as US, South Korea begin their 1st joint military exercise of Trump's 2nd term
item.description: North Korea fired several ballistic missiles into the sea after South Korea and the U.S. kicked off their first major joint military exercise of President Donald Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US-flagged tanker collides with container ship near UK
item.description: Thirty-two people were brought ashore, conditions unknown, after a vessel collided with a tanker carrying Jet-A1 fuel causing multiple explosions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New study shuts down ICC charges against Israel over Gaza starvation claims
item.description: A new study contradicts ICC claims of Israeli-induced starvation in Gaza, showing sufficient food supply while exposing Hamas' manipulation of aid distribution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syria's worst violence in months reopens wounds of the civil war
item.description: An ambush on a Syrian security patrol by gunmen loyal to ousted leader Bashar Assad escalated into clashes that a war monitor estimates have killed more than 1,000 people over four days.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope thanks volunteers for 'miracle of tenderness' as another Holy Year event passes without him
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis thanked volunteers Sunday for the “miracle of tenderness” they offer the sick, as he continued his treatment for double pneumonia and doctors reported some positive news.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Romania blocks frontrunner from postponed presidential race
item.description: Protests have erupted in Romania after authorities barred Calin Georgescu, a candidate critical of NATO, from running in May's presidential election re-run.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ontario will tariff electricity going to 3 US states on Monday, premier says
item.description: The leader said new tariffs are scheduled to be implemented Monday in an effort to push back on Trump’s international economic measures.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: See it: Activists vandalize Trump's Scotland golf resort
item.description: Pro-Palestinian activists said Saturday they vandalized one of Trump's golf resorts in Scotland in response to his proposal to empty the Gaza Strip of its Palestinian population.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In his own words: Pope Francis' views on resigning changed over time
item.description: ROME (AP) — As he recovers from pneumonia in the hospital, Pope Francis has increasingly handed off his day-to-day responsibilities to cardinals as questions swirl about the near and long-term future of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Scotland golf resort vandalized by pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: A pro-Palestinian activist group has claimed responsibility for the crimes, which included defacing a building and digging up greens.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies as US cuts its access to satellite images
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia attacked Ukrainian energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones during the night, officials said Friday, hobbling the country’s ability to deliver heat and light to its citizens and to power weapons factories vital to its defenses.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, WGN 9

item.title: EU leaders commit to working together after Trump signals that Europe must defend itself
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders on Thursday committed to working together to bolster the continent's defenses and to free up hundreds of billions of euros for security after U.S. President Donald Trump’s repeated warnings that he would cut them adrift to face the threat of Russia alone.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump NATO stance shows 'simple-minded view of the world': Bolton
item.description: Trump said the U.S. may not help defend NATO members that fail to meet their military spending commitment.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation
